description:
You are a Tier-1 support engineer who has just pulled a customer’s on-premise log bundle into the lab VM.  
    One of the files,  
        /home/user/logs/incident.log  
    already exists on disk.  
  
    Management wants a quick breakdown of how many times every *status / error code* appears inside that log.  
    The codes are the second whitespace-separated field on each line, e.g.  
  
        [2023-05-10 10:01:02] ERR42 Disk failure.  
  
    1. Parse every line of /home/user/logs/incident.log.  
    2. Extract only the codes (the second field).  
    3. Produce a frequency table that is **numerically sorted in descending order of occurrence**.  
       • If two codes have the same count, keep them in normal ASCII/lexicographic order.  
    4. Save the result to  
        /home/user/diagnostics/error_code_frequency.txt  
  
    Exact output format (what the automated grader will check):  
      • One code per line.  
      • Code, a single TAB character (`\t`), then the integer count.  
      • No leading or trailing spaces.  
      • A single trailing newline at the very end of the file.  
  
    Example of the expected layout (illustrative only; the exact numbers must come from the real file):  
        CODE123<TAB>7\n  
        CODE999<TAB>3\n  
        ALPHA01<TAB>1\n  
  
    IMPORTANT:  
      • Use the classic command-line utilities “sort” and “uniq” to do the counting (awk/cut may be used for field extraction).  
      • Do **not** change the original /home/user/logs/incident.log file.  
      • Make sure the target directory (/home/user/diagnostics) exists before you write the output file.  
      • When you are done, the only new file created by your commands must be /home/user/diagnostics/error_code_frequency.txt and it must follow the format above.  
      • Feel free to display the resulting table to the console, but the grader will only inspect the file on disk.  
  
    Once the file is in place with the correct contents and format, the task is complete.
truth:
Pre-populated artefacts on the VM before the agent starts:  
  
    1. File: /home/user/logs/incident.log  
       Contents (10 lines, each ending in '\n'):  
        [2023-05-10 10:00:00] OK System started.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:01:02] ERR42 Disk failure.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:02:05] WARN27 High memory usage.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:03:07] ERR42 Disk failure.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:04:10] ERR99 Network unreachable.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:05:12] ERR42 Disk failure.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:06:15] WARN27 High memory usage.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:07:17] ERR99 Network unreachable.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:08:20] INFO11 User login.\n  
        [2023-05-10 10:09:22] ERR42 Disk failure.\n  
  
    2. Directory /home/user/diagnostics does *not* exist initially. The agent must create it with default user permissions (mkdir).  
  
    Expected artefacts *after* successful task execution:  
  
    1. File: /home/user/diagnostics/error_code_frequency.txt  
       Exact contents (5 lines, each ending in '\n'):  
        ERR42\t4\n  
        ERR99\t2\n  
        WARN27\t2\n  
        INFO11\t1\n  
        OK\t1\n
name: task_000000_ca56bd9d
